title: Ixora tidorensis
intro: Ixora tidorensis, also known as the Tidorea, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ixora tidorensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ixora tidorensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of extracts and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Ixora tidorensis belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Ixora. Ixora tidorensis is a flowering plant native to the Tidore Island in Indonesia. The plant is known for its vibrant flowers in shades of pink, purple, and white. Ixora tidorensis is often cultivated for its ornamental value in tropical gardens.
